sanskrit:
नियतस्य तु सन्न्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते।
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः॥
transliteration:
niyatasya tu sannyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate |
mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Of prescribed action, renunciation is not valid; from delusion, its abandonment is declared to be in the mode of darkness.
contextual_meaning: Abandoning necessary life-tasks out of confusion or escapism is a psychological defense, not true renunciation.
psychological_state: Avoidant-dismissive attachment: the mind labels legitimate duties as burdens to justify withdrawal.
trigger_scenario: Quitting your job or studies because it feels ‘spiritual’ while bills pile up and relationships fray.
anchor_text: Don’t quit life’s duties—face them with clarity, not confusion.
